title: Firefighter

rationales.taskAutomation: Suppression, rescue, and emergency medical response are physical, unstructured, and licensed acts that AI cannot perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Predictive-dispatch routing and drone/thermal-imaging tools exist and help incident commanders, but nothing role-specific has reached the standard toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Fire departments are conservative, budget-limited public employers; adoption of even mature dispatch-support tools is still early-adopter only.
rationales.agenticExposure: Firefighting is entirely physical-world work; autonomous software agents have no path into suppression or rescue.

tasksAtRisk: Post-incident report and NFIRS data entry
tasksGrowing: Live incident command decision-making under uncertainty
Public fire-safety education and community engagement
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-2011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Technical rescue and hazmat response certification
Incident command software and predictive-dispatch tool literacy

insight: Firefighting sits at the physical floor of this index: suppression and rescue can't be automated at any reliability AI offers today. The only real AI pressure is on the administrative tail of the job.
